question.text: Under SEBI IA Regulations, what is the maximum period for which an investment adviser can collect advisory fees in advance from a client?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under SEBI IA Regulations 2013, an investment adviser can collect fees in advance for a maximum period of 1 year (12 months). This applies to the fee-only model mandated for investment advisers, where the IA's only source of income from clients is the advisory fee.
**The Trap:** Candidates may confuse the advance fee period with contract tenure. The advisory agreement may last longer than 1 year, but the IA cannot collect fees for more than 1 year in advance at any given time.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Fee structures and limits are central to NISM Series X-A. Compare with portfolio management fee models (Series XII) which have different structures.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Collect fees quarterly or semi-annually even though you can charge up to 1 year in advance. This reduces refund complications if the client terminates the engagement early.
